It belongs to the genus Guapeba, which consists of several species of trees and shrubs native to the tropical regions of South America. The classification of Guapeba gomphiifolia is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Magnoliids, class - Magnoliopsida, order - Magnoliales, family - Annonaceae, genus - Guapeba, and species - G. gomphiifolia. Guapeba gomphiifolia is closely related to other Annonaceae species, such as the custard apple and the soursop.
